meaning: n.接受,承诺,容忍,赞同,相信
examples: The new laws have gained widespread acceptance.
The acceptance of new members is strictly controlled.
Social acceptance is important for most young people.
The hotel claims that William Faulkner wrote his Nobel Prize acceptance speech there in 1950.
Viewers' "chances of acceptance or rejection" must be permanently shown and updated at no more than 10-minute intervals.
